explanation: The contract can receive ether (it has payable functions or a receive/fallback) but exposes no function that sends ether back out. Any ether that reaches the contract, whether by a payable call, a selfdestruct force send, or a coinbase reward, is trapped forever because there is no transfer, send, call, or selfdestruct path to move it.
impact: All ether sent to the contract is permanently frozen: users lose deposited funds with no recovery path, and no admin action or upgrade of a non upgradeable contract can get them back.
